implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: UI form for adding a new task, collects title, description, priority, and due date. It still does not include a user name field.
implementation.name: TaskForm
implementation.pseudo_code:
Render a form with fields:
  - Title (required)
  - Description
  - Priority (Low / Medium / High)
  - Due Date
On submit:
  - Validate required fields
  - Call onSubmit with task data

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Handles submission from TaskForm and calls the backend API to create a task. Because the form does not collect a user name, none is included here.
implementation.name: handleCreateTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
When TaskForm submits:
  - Call api.createTask with task data (no user name)
  - Reload tasks from backend
  - Close the form

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Makes POST request to /tasks with title, description, priority, due date, and completed flag. User name is not included.
implementation.name: api.createTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
Send POST request to /tasks with task data
IF response is OK:
  - Return created task
ELSE:
  - Throw error

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: FastAPI POST /tasks endpoint that receives task fields and passes them to database.add_task. User name is not included or validated.
implementation.name: create_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
On POST /tasks:
  - Receive TaskCreate payload
  - Call database.add_task with provided fields
  - Return created Task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Creates and persists a new task record. User name is not expected or stored.
implementation.name: add_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
Read current tasks
Create new Task with unique ID and provided fields
Append new task to list
Save list
Return new Task

missing_implementations: User name field is not collected in the UI form.
User name is not passed from the frontend to the backend.
Backend endpoint does not accept or validate user name.
Database logic does not store user name.
Validation for missing user name is not implemented as required by acceptance criteria.
